statement.p: A restaurant made 400 cups of pudding for a festival.
Customers at the festival will be served {{\frac{1}{6}}} of a cup of pudding per serving. How many customers can the restaurant serve at the festival with the 400 cups of pudding?
The restaurant can serve customers at the festival with the 400 cups of pudding.

solution.p: There are a total of 400 cups of pudding, with each serving being {{\frac{1}{6}}} cup. To find how many servings can be served, we need to find how many {{\frac{1}{6}}} cups are there in 400 cups, a division problem:
The restaurant can serve {2400} customers at the festival with the 400 cups of pudding.
Since each serving has {{\frac{1}{6}}} cup, we know each cup has 6 servings. So 400 cups has 400\cdot6={2400} servings.
